examples: More recent geologic studies documented deglaciation and the existence of ice-free areas throughout major coastal areas of British Columbia, Canada, by 13,000 years ago.
Although change in carbon dioxide content closely follows change in temperature during periods of deglaciation, it apparently lags behind temperature during periods of cooling.
Can deglaciation trigger earthquakes in N. America?
There is another possibility warranting consideration as well, one involving slight differences in Arctic and Antarctic deglaciation time cycles.
